title: Ensure that the audit logs are forwarded off the cluster for retention

input_row.title: Ensure that the audit logs are forwarded off the cluster for retention
input_row.description: Retain the logs for at least 30 days or as appropriate.
input_row.rationale: Retaining logs for at least 30 days ensures that you can go back in time and investigate or correlate any events. Set your audit log retention period to 30 days or as per your business requirements.
input_row.audit: OpenShift audit works at the API server level, logging all requests coming to the server. Audit is on by default. Best practice is to ship audit logs off the cluster for retention. OpenShift includes the optional Cluster Logging operator and the elasticSearch operator. OpenShift cluster logging can be configured to send logs to destinations outside of your OpenShift Container Platform cluster instead of the default elasticsearch log store using the following methods: • Sending logs using the fluentd forward protocol. You can create a ConfigMap to use the fluentdForward protocol to securely send logs to an external logging aggregator that accepts the fluentdForward protocol. • Sending logs using syslog. You can create a ConfigMap to use the syslog protocol to send logs to an external syslog (RFC 3164) server. Alternatively, you can use the Log Forwarding API, currently in Technology Preview. The Log Forwarding API, which is easier to configure than the fluentdForward protocol and syslog, exposes configuration for sending logs to the internal elasticsearch log store and to external fluentd log aggregation solutions. You cannot use the ConfigMap methods and the Log Forwarding API in the same cluster. Verify that audit log forwarding is configured as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Follow the documentation for log forwarding. Forwarding logs to third party systems . Internal Only - General

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit process involves verifying the configuration and functionality of audit log forwarding. Based on the information provided, it indicates that audit logs in OpenShift can indeed be monitored and configured through IBM Cloud APIs/CLI. Best practices and methods for log forwarding are specified, suggesting that this is a standard IBM Cloud service property tied to logging, further supporting the ability for automated checks.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_cluster_audit_log_forwarding_configured

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check:
- Use the IBM Cloud CLI command `ibmcloud oc cluster get` to retrieve the cluster details and configuration.
- Query the OpenShift object for ConfigMaps using the command `oc get configmap -n openshift-logging` to check the existence of configurations related to fluentd forwarding or syslog configurations.
- Verify that the logs are being sent to an external log aggregator by inspecting log forwarding configuration settings.
- Specifically check if the Log Forwarding API has been implemented and correctly configured by querying the relevant API endpoints for the Log Forwarding settings.
  
**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- Pass: If the audit log forwarding configuration exists and is correctly set to forward logs to an appropriate external system (fluentd, syslog, or via the Log Forwarding API).
- Fail: If there is no configuration present or if it defaults to internal logging without any indication of external forwarding.

This automated approach will ensure compliance with the control by leveraging the capabilities of IBM Cloud APIs to check the required configurations.
